bird.name: Northern saw-whet owl
bird.summary: The northern saw-whet owl (Aegolius acadicus) is a species of small owl in the family Strigidae. The species is native to North America.

locations.name: 北美洲
locations.summary: 北亞美利加洲（英語：North America；字源：亞美利哥·維斯普奇），簡稱北美洲，位於西半球北部（或北半球）。東臨大西洋，西臨太平洋，北瀕北冰洋，南以巴拿馬運河為界與南美洲劃分。北美洲還包括加勒比海中眾多島嶼（主要為西印度群島）。北…

relations.subject: Northern saw-whet owl
relations.evidence: The species is native to North America

relations.subject: Northern saw-whet owl
relations.evidence: Most birds nest in dense coniferous forests near wetlands during the breeding season, but may also breed in mixed-deciduous forests at the southern edge of their range in the eastern United States.Rasmussen, J.L., S.G
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。
